mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in real retail sales reflects persistent consumer spending strength that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following months with RSAFS below its 12-month moving average are not negative on average, or the sign consistency of positive RSAFS months falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy weighs on equity valuations by raising the opportunity cost of capital, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.0%, or the average return in triggered periods exceeds the average return in non-triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and increase in payroll employment signals a tight labor market that boosts wage-driven consumption and corporate pricing power, jointly producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of falling UNRATE and rising PAYEMS fails to produce positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of historical episodes, or the interaction term in a bivariate regression shows an insignificant coefficient (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, accelerating consumer price inflation forces the central bank to tighten more aggressively than priced, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During months classified as monetary tightening cycles, positive 20-day S&P 500 returns follow rising CPIAUCSL in more than 50% of observations, or the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and forward returns is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth indicates expanding manufacturing activity and capacity utilization that supports earnings momentum for industrial and energy sectors, lifting aggregate S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and deteriorating consumer confidence tied to household wealth effects, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Months with declining HOUST do not produce negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or the hit rate of negative returns falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 4.5 percent signals a tight labor market that raises wage cost pressures and squeezes corporate margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE < 4.5%, or average returns in triggered months are higher than in non-triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects broad-based labor income expansion that underpins aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in PAYEMS are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of historical observations, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS changes and forward returns is negative over any 3-year window.
